attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; operates as a leadout specialist who positions for bunch sprints. When going for personal result, relies on final 200m positioning rather than early moves.
team_tactics: Serves as final leadout man in BORA sprint train, delivering designated sprinter (Bennett) with precision timing before either peeling off or contesting finish himself if legs permit.
terrain_preference: Pure flat sprint stages where leadout power matters most. Struggles when terrain includes meaningful climbing that saps leadout capacity and questions resource allocation.
vulnerability: Climbing capacity limits effectiveness on hillier sprint stages; team's heavy leadout investment sometimes misallocated when gradient undermines his finishing capability.
key_patterns: Dual-role execution: delivers leadout for Bennett then contests top-10 finishes himself (2 instances)
Leadout dominance allows celebration before finish line, indicating power surplus in sprint trains (1 dominant execution)
Climbing limitations create mismatch between team leadout investment and actual finishing capacity (1 clear example)
Exceptional judgment on leadout timing and placement, evidenced by both team and personal results
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong leadout execution evident but personal results (4th, 10th) suggest climbing limitations prevent converting team investment into wins
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 13
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
